release_body:
* fixed issue with bash-preexec
* fixed issue with pushover priority

There aren't any breaking changes in this release but due to 
the size I've bumped the major version number.

- api: `pywal` can now be imported and used as a module.
    - See [using pywal as a module](https://github.com/dylanaraps/pywal/wiki/Using-%60pywal%60-as-a-module).
- args: Added `-a` to control transparency. (URxvt only)
- comments: Removed redundent comments.
- linting: Removed lint comments.
- reload: Reload `polybar` colors automatically.
- speed: Sped up colorscheme generation by `5-6`x  by resizing image before processing.
- tests: Added more tests.
- util: Files are now saved safely.
    - Parent dirs are created on file save if they don't exist.

Features

* You can now open long posts/comments with the $PAGER by pressing `l`.
* Changed a couple of visual separators.

Documentation

* Added testing instructions to the FAQ.
* Read feature files as utf8

* Adds pytz as a library requirement (#36)
* Adds Flaky library for testing (#42)
* Logs traceback errors onto the RequestLog model (#18)

NOTE: New features expand on the current feature set. However, no current features should break. Upgrade accordingly.

Thanks to ( , @jameshiew, @aschn )

* Providing proper access to ``Repo.active`` when this is not returned from API.
* Providing proper access to ``Job.duration`` when this is not returned from API.
Expands documentation and a streamlined some small pieces of code.

Version 0.4.0
-------------------

- Breaking change: Removed 'dnd' from functions for clearer 'dry_run' or 'delete_on_success'
- Breaking change: Removing 'dangerzone' file, moving 'reuse' function to root namespace
- Added management tools for Firefox and Chrome cookies
- Added unique wrapper tool, ensures return value has not been returned before
- Changed all top level imports to have underscore before them like standard library

* 1.0.1 - Mar 15 2016
- Remove debugging print
- Note that it has been explicitly tested on 2.7, 3.4, and 3.5 in README.
- Add link to pydoc in README

* Bug fixes in `Surface` classes
* Added tests
* AppVeyor and Travis-CI integration
* Documentation updates
* Style updates and code reformatting after pylint

You can install the latest version via `pip install NURBS-Python`

### How-to run the tests

All included test can be run by executing `pytest` from the command prompt or using your favorite IDE. To install the required packages for testing, please use `pip install pytest`.

- Fix #23
- Improve multi-byte character support
- Bug fixes

Stop dynamically referecing logger.
Disable internal logging by default and reduce logging verbosity
See [README.rst](README.rst).

* Require path setting to the Sqlite database
    (the previous default value ":memory:" doesn't help, see #5)
  * Fix a Python3 incompatibility (#3)

    incompatibility (3)

For details see https://pypi.python.org/pypi/python-logstash-async/1.2.0
See [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/macropin/django-registration/blob/v1.6/CHANGELOG) for release notes.

In this release (1.10.0) we implemented:

* Tags support
* Better pagination support
* Human readable classes
* general bug fixes

A spacial thanks goes to: @Ormod, @jwilk, @andrewsomething, @moeseth, @cprogrammer1994, @goodcode and everybody else reporting bugs and pushing for new PR to improve this library!
This release adds IPv6 support to the lease file parsing and the new Lease6 class to represent a IPv6 lease.
* Bugfix to allow compound local imports (see #13 - thanks @mhotwagner)

- add statistics for item-matrix
- use title for item-list/matrix/tree in an admonition
- titles for columns in item-matrix
- include external relationships in item-matrix when no specific relation (=wildcard all) is given
- sanity check: no relation to self for items
- showcasing all of this in the example

release_name: Keep available currencies as a list

release_body:
* [#371](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-python/pull/371) Add accessor `last_response` on `StripeObject` for accessing request ID and other metadata

See [the changelog for more details](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-python/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md).

**New API**
- support https://github.com/aliyun/aliyun-log-cli/issues/13 by adding api `get_log_all` and `pull_log`

**Improvement**
- ignore errors when there's invalid utf8 char in log response e.g. `\x8b`.

**Improvement**
- support https support #57 
- support configure web tracking of logstore #58 

**Bug fixed**
- support issue in update logstore #48
See [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/SAP/cf-python-logging-support/blob/v3.2.0/CHANGELOG.md#320---2018-05-31)

## Google Compute Engine
- Improve hostname setting and rsyslog daemon reset when using the dhcp exit hook.

## Google Compute Engine OS Login
- The [OS Login](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/instances/managing-instance-access) feature is generally available.
- Change the OS Login uid restriction to allow uid 1000.
- Close socket connections after requesting metadata.

## 2.0.1 (2017-10-21)
- Allow s3.Bucket AccessControl to be an AWSHelperFn
- Add AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::ListenerCertificate
- Add serverless FunctionName and change how Tags are implemented
- Make AdjustmentType an optional property of ScalingPolicy as it is not used/supported for target (#849)
- Add maintenance window for SSM (#851)
- Add Tags, Tracing, KmsKeyArn, DLQ to serverless(SAM) (#853)
- Add new AWS::SSM resources (#854)
- EC2 NoDevice should be type boolean not dict (#858)
- Fixes RecordColumns cardinality for InputSchema and ReferenceSchema (#859)
- Make AWS::Batch::JobQueue::JobQueueName optional (#860)
- Fixes ApplicationOutput/Output cardinality (#863)
Various optims and bug fixes

# Changelog
- Various CI improvements

## Bugfixes
- Prevent GSSError/\_display\_status() infinite recursion (#112)

## Documentation
- Correct name of HTTP service (#107)

* Fix bytes/str comparison in JWE
* Test under Python 3.6

#### New features

* Add support ESP stored templates and batch sending/merge.
  Exact capabilities vary widely by ESP -- be sure to read the notes for your ESP.
  [docs](http://anymail.readthedocs.io/en/v0.3/sending/templates/)
* Add pre_send and post_send signals.
  [docs](http://anymail.readthedocs.io/en/v0.3/sending/signals/)

#### Other changes

* **Mandrill:** add support for esp_extra; deprecate Mandrill-specific
  message attributes left over from Djrill.
  See [migrating from Djrill](http://anymail.readthedocs.io/en/v0.3/esps/mandrill/#migrating-from-djrill).
